positions.name: iShares Core S&P 500 UCITS ETF (Acc)
positions.thesis: Core broad market exposure to S&P 500. IE-domiciled accumulating structure eliminates 30% WHT on distributions — structurally optimal vehicle for a Lebanese resident. Portfolio anchor. MAY 3 UPDATE: Position at 39.42% of total NAV (2.1843 shares, $1,703.34 market value at $779.81/share, +7.23% unrealized). Confirmed: $142.09 unconditional tranche deployed at April 28 open — position grew from 1.9989 to 2.1843 shares. May 3 hard cash deployment deadline has been met. S&P 500 hit record 7,230.12 on May 1 — sixth consecutive weekly gain. Nasdaq at all-time high 25,114.44. No structural invalidation conditions triggered. $145.2B AUM, 0.07% TER. Thesis conviction: STRONGER.
invalidation_conditions: Structural shift away from US equities as the dominant long-term growth engine
A superior WHT-efficient broad market vehicle becomes available

positions.name: Amazon.com Inc
positions.thesis: Conviction dip buy entered during Liberation Day tariff shock. Core thesis: AWS cloud dominance (~33% global cloud market share), advertising revenue at double-digit growth, and AI infrastructure investment (Bedrock, Trainium custom silicon). MAY 3 UPDATE: Q1 2026 earnings delivered April 29 — massive beat on all metrics. Revenue $181.5B (+17% YoY, beat $177.2B estimate). EPS $2.78 vs $1.63 consensus (massively above). AWS grew 28% YoY to $37.6B — 15-quarter high, beat 26% estimate. Advertising +24% YoY to $17.24B. Amazon chips (Graviton, Trainium, Nitro) exceeded $20B annual revenue run rate at triple-digit YoY growth. Post-earnings concern: $200B FY2026 total capex guidance and FCF collapse to $1.2B TTM (from $25.9B) on elevated property and equipment investment. Stock at $268.26 (+25.40% unrealized from $213.94 cost basis). All ADD criteria technically met but capital directed to higher-conviction NVDA pre-earnings play instead. Thesis conviction: STRONGER.
invalidation_conditions: AWS loses meaningful cloud market share to Azure or GCP on a sustained basis
Regulatory breakup that separates AWS from the retail/advertising business
Sustained margin compression across all segments (retail, advertising, AND cloud simultaneously)
FCF remains compressed below $5B TTM for 2+ consecutive quarters while capex stays above $150B annualized — reassess capital efficiency thesis

positions.name: SPDR Gold Shares
positions.thesis: Macro hedge against dollar weakness, geopolitical risk, and inflationary pressure. Gold has no cash flows so carries zero WHT exposure despite US domicile. MAY 3 UPDATE — WATCH FLAG ELEVATED, CONVICTION WEAKER, TRIGGER IMMINENT: GLD at $423.18 (May 1 XML mark), buffer above $420 hard trim trigger has collapsed to only $3.18 (0.76%) — down from 3.1% on April 27. CRITICAL: The $420 daily close trigger is at immediate risk of firing. HORMUZ THESIS INTACT: Dual blockade confirmed — US blockading Iranian ports, Iran restricting transit. Ship transits collapsed 95% (130/day to 6/day). IEA confirmed largest supply disruption in global oil market history. GLD 4-week invalidation clock has NOT started. BULLISH SUPPORTS: Central bank demand ~850 tonnes 2026 forecast, stagflation backdrop (CPI +3.3% YoY), JPMorgan $6,300/oz year-end target (raised), Fed uncertainty (Powell term expiry). BEARISH DRAG: Morgan Stanley cut to $5,200/oz. Price action this week (-2.3% from $433.25) is concerning despite intact macro pillars. HARD RULE: If ANY daily close below $420 — immediately trim 25% of GLD (~$106) into CSPX, no discretion. Thesis conviction: WEAKER.
invalidation_conditions: Sustained USD strengthening cycle driven by structurally higher real rates that persist above inflation
Major reversal in central bank gold buying trend (multiple major central banks becoming net sellers)
GLD breaks and holds below $420 on a daily close — triggers immediate 25% trim (~$106) into CSPX — TRIGGER IMMINENT as of May 3 ($3.18 buffer)
Iran war fully and durably resolved AND Strait of Hormuz formally reopened AND risk-on environment maintained for 4+ consecutive weeks — 4-week clock NOT started as of May 3
Morgan Stanley reduced target ($5,200/oz) combined with continued institutional ETF outflows for 2+ more consecutive months — would escalate to DETERIORATING

positions.name: Mastercard Inc
positions.wht_exposure: 30% WHT on dividend — confirmed $3.33 withheld YTD (MA dividend only). Annualised WHT drag <$5 at current position size. Immaterial.
positions.thesis: Conviction dip buy during Liberation Day volatility. Thesis: payments network duopoly (MA + Visa) with near-monopoly economics, asset-light model generating high ROE, global consumer spending volume exposure, and pricing power. MAY 3 UPDATE — CONVICTION WEAKER, WATCH FLAG: Q1 2026 earnings reported April 30. EPS $4.60 vs $4.41 expected (beat). Revenue $8.4B (+16% YoY, beat). However: cross-border volume grew only +13% YoY — BELOW the 14% ADD threshold. ADD trigger did NOT fire. More concerning: April cross-border decelerated sharply to +9% YoY, and travel cross-border dropped from +8% to +2% YoY — Middle East conflict is materially suppressing MA's highest-margin revenue category. MA also acquired BVNK for $1.5B in a stablecoin deal (capital allocation risk in near-term). Current price $495.46 (-3.46% unrealized from $513.24 cost basis). Reducing position by ~$75 to reflect lowered conviction. Thesis conviction: WEAKER.
invalidation_conditions: Disintermediation by real-time payment rails (FedNow, open banking) gaining scale at the expense of card networks
Regulatory caps on interchange fees in core US or European markets
Cross-border volume remains below 10% in Q2 2026 earnings (late July) — escalate to DETERIORATING and evaluate full exit
Sustained global consumer spending contraction lasting multiple quarters
April cross-border trend at 9% worsens further in May/June — Iran conflict creating structural suppression of travel spending

positions.name: NVIDIA Corporation
positions.thesis: AI infrastructure buildout driving unprecedented GPU demand. NVDA holds dominant position in AI training and inference hardware; CUDA ecosystem creates deep switching costs. MAY 3 UPDATE: Current price $198.45 (+9.06% unrealized from $181.98 cost basis). Pulled back from a recent higher ATH (likely ~$248-$250 based on 20% monthly rally data). EARNINGS DATE CONFIRMED: May 20, 2026 for Q1 FY2027 — resolves the prior May 20 vs May 27 discrepancy. Consensus: $78.8B revenue / $1.75 EPS. Vera Rubin AI servers confirmed in full production (3.3x speed improvement over Blackwell Ultra). $1T+ combined Blackwell AND Vera Rubin backlog through end-2027. NEW RISK: KeyBanc projects 25% reduction in 2026 Rubin GPU production targets due to HBM memory shortages from SK Hynix and other suppliers — production timing risk, not demand invalidation. Average analyst target ~$268 (35%+ upside from $198.45). 38+ analysts Strong Buy consensus. Hyperscalers committed $600B+ in 2026 AI capex. AMZN chips ($20B+ annual run rate) validates the hyperscaler AI hardware spending cycle. Adding $150 pre-May 20 earnings as highest-conviction near-term catalyst play. Thesis conviction: STRONGEST IN PORTFOLIO.
invalidation_conditions: AMD or custom silicon (Google TPU, Amazon Trainium, Microsoft Maia) takes meaningful and sustained share in AI training workloads
AI capex cycle reverses — hyperscalers visibly cut or guide down infrastructure spend across multiple quarters
Export restrictions to China expand materially beyond current guidance, reducing total addressable market by >15%
Post-GTC 2026: $1T+ order backlog proves overstated or a significant portion of orders cancelled in subsequent quarters
Vera Rubin production delays extending beyond Q3 2026 — would defer the next re-rating catalyst
HBM shortage worsens materially: if Rubin unit cuts exceed 40% and management guides down Q2 FY2027 revenue on May 20 call — re-evaluate conviction

positions.name: iShares USD Treasury Bond 0-3 Month UCITS ETF (Acc)
positions.thesis: Cash equivalent / capital preservation layer with 0% effective WHT. Replaced SGOV (which carried 30% WHT on distributions) — structurally optimal for Lebanese residents. US Treasury interest paid to an Irish-domiciled fund qualifies for the portfolio interest exemption from NRA withholding. Near-zero duration risk (0-3M). MAY 3 UPDATE: Position at 13.92% of total NAV (5.6247 shares at $106.94, $601.51 market value, -1.07% unrealized). YTM ~3.67% — well above 3.0% trim trigger. FOMC DOVISH SURPRISE: April 28-29 FOMC held at 3.50-3.75% but with 8-4 vote — Governor Miran dissented for 25bps cut, the most divided FOMC since October 1992. This accelerates the stage-down timeline. June 16-17 FOMC is the next stage-down trigger review. FIRST STAGE-DOWN: Trimming $75 (~0.70 shares) this week per the active plan, reducing from 13.92% to ~12.2% of NAV. Target is ~10% of NAV through staged reductions conditional on continued dovish signals. Iran-Hormuz closure reinforces capital preservation rationale for remaining balance. Thesis conviction: SAME.
invalidation_conditions: Fed cuts rates aggressively (>100bps in 2026) — opportunity cost vs equities rises significantly, accelerate trim to 8%
A better WHT-efficient fixed income vehicle with superior risk-adjusted return is identified
Portfolio NAV grows large enough (>$10K) that maintaining a 10%+ cash equivalent layer imposes unacceptable drag on long-term returns
YTM falls below 3.0% — at that point, reduce toward 5-8% of NAV immediately
